prompt: Our NoteSync project docs have grown organically — everything's dumped into flat files under docs/ and the README has become a catch-all. Can you reorganize the documentation into a proper structure? The README should be minimal, and we need a clear separation between what features do and how they're implemented.

assertions.text: docs/functional/index.md exists with links to feature specs
docs/technical/index.md exists with links to technical docs
README.md is under 15 lines (minimal with link to docs)
Functional specs contain no code blocks (SQL, Clojure, or Dockerfile)
Technical docs exist for sync/real-time architecture
No content duplicated between README and docs/index.md
docs/index.md favors category indexes over leaf docs
